title: ED procedural sedation documentation prompt
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports an auditable sedation proforma, competency, staffing, equipment, adverse-event reporting and recording without selecting a sedative or dose.
assessment.rationale: The exact RCEM governance section supports patient concerns, clinician risk context, manual focused assessment, injury or illness severity, appearance, and an auditable documentation plan only when assessed; it does not select sedation or a medicine.

source.title: Procedural Sedation in the Emergency Department
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE credentialing, medicine and facility policy govern local use
source.population: Adults and children, excluding neonates, undergoing procedural sedation in an emergency department.
source.setting: Emergency department procedural sedation governance, pre-sedation assessment, monitoring and post-sedation review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting use of a sedation proforma, clinician assessment of patient and procedure factors, staffing and environment, monitoring, adverse events and clinician post-sedation review. Pharmacological agents and doses were explicitly excluded from mappings.

exact_location.heading: Summary, governance and auditable sedation record

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports an auditable sedation proforma, competency, staffing, equipment, adverse-event reporting and recording without selecting a sedative or dose.

assessment.text: Supports documenting clinician-assessed patient/procedure factors, airway and aspiration risk, planned level, monitoring, adverse events and clinician review after sedation. Medicine and dose tables are excluded.
assessment.rationale: The exact assessment and monitoring section supports recording measured vital data, time-critical abnormalities, existing results, completed risk/consent discussion, specialist involvement and post-sedation handover only when actually performed.
